executive_summary: Store total sales Inc VAT for week ending 2026-05-16 were 368,436.45 with an overall achieved margin of 17.53%, which requires investigation against budgeted margin targets.

Total department P&L as reported shows a combined loss of -43,606.51 on departmental sales of 62,099.87, driven almost entirely by a catastrophic Cigarettes CoS anomaly of 101,676.87 against sales of only 1,284.42, suggesting a critical stock file, opening stock, or data input error that must be resolved immediately.

Total waste for the week was 8,111.35, representing 2.39% of sales ex VAT; this is a material figure with Deli (1,253.18), Provisions (1,136.11), Fish (862.65), and Produce (1,393.82) as the four largest waste contributors accounting for approximately 56% of total waste.

Multiple departments are reporting margins well in excess of 100% including Lamb at 101.91%, Bacon & Loose S.R.P at 117.90%, Produce at 112.12%, Poultry at 98.93%, and Fish at 99.44%; these are not credible achieved margins and point to stock count errors, missing closing stock entries, or incorrect purchase postings across these departments.

The Deli department has no closing stock recorded, a CoS equal to its full opening stock of 3,715.48, and has generated a loss of -2,226.96 at a margin of -149.61%; a closing stock count must be completed and posted without delay.

Scan margins across the majority of departments are materially lower than reported P&L margins, which is a further indicator that the P&L figures this week are unreliable and stock-count-driven rather than reflective of true trading performance.

The gap between departmental total sales of 62,099.87 and store total sales ex VAT of 340,018.57 indicates that not all departments are included in the detailed P&L breakdown provided; the overall 17.53% margin should be used as the primary trading benchmark pending full P&L reconciliation.
biggest_wins: Poultry delivered the strongest sales contribution in the department breakdown at 25,390.16, with a scan margin of 16.41% which, while lower than the overstated P&L margin, indicates solid trading performance and tight stock management given a low CoS of 271.12.

Provisions recorded sales of 4,358.19 with a very low CoS of 55.65 and a scan margin of 19.71%, suggesting well-controlled stock levels, minimal waste exposure at 1,136.11 notwithstanding, and strong sell-through of fresh provisions lines.

Beef generated sales of 12,520.90 with a scan margin of 20.51%, the highest credible scan margin in the meat categories, and waste of 84.07 which is well controlled relative to the sales volume, indicating good ordering and rotation discipline.

Fish achieved sales of 6,083.82 with a scan margin of 19.18%; despite high waste of 862.65, the underlying scan margin is acceptable and suggests the category is trading well when stock file integrity is applied.
Produce posted sales of 6,547.11 with a scan margin of 19.13% and, while the negative CoS of -793.52 flags a stock count issue, the scan margin performance is in line with department expectations.
biggest_risks: Cigarettes: The opening stock of 102,500.77 against a closing stock of 823.90 and sales of only 1,284.42 has produced a CoS of 101,676.87 and a loss of -100,392.45 at a margin of -7,816.16%; this is unequivocally a data or stock entry error and represents the single highest priority issue in this report. A full Cigarettes stock count and stock file audit must be completed before the next trading day.

Deli: No closing stock has been recorded for the week. The full opening stock value of 3,715.48 has fallen through as CoS, producing a loss of -2,226.96 at -149.61% margin. Waste of 1,253.18 is also the second highest waste figure in the store. A closing stock count is mandatory and the waste figure must be reviewed against production records.

Provisions: Waste of 1,136.11 against sales of 4,358.19 represents a waste rate of approximately 26% within this department, which is critically high. Immediate review of ordering volumes, production quantities, date code management, and markdown activity is required.

Fish: Waste of 862.65 against sales of 6,083.82 equates to a departmental waste rate of approximately 14.2%, which is significantly above acceptable thresholds for a fresh fish counter or chilled fish department. Ordering frequency, portion sizing, and end-of-day markdown protocols must be reviewed.

Produce: Negative CoS of -793.52 indicates that closing stock exceeds opening stock by 793.52 without corresponding purchase postings being visible in the data. This suggests either purchases were not posted during the week or a stock count discrepancy exists. This must be reconciled before the next stock count.

Lamb and Bacon & Loose S.R.P: Both departments are reporting negative CoS figures (-30.33 and -201.47 respectively), which is arithmetically impossible in normal trading and points to stock count overstatement on closing stock, unposted credits, or data entry errors that will distort the following week's figures.

Overall scan margin vs P&L margin disconnect: The majority of fresh departments are showing P&L margins between 88% and 118% versus scan margins of 11% to 25%. This scale of divergence across multiple departments in a single week indicates systemic stock count reliability issues that will undermine period-end P&L accuracy if not addressed.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Opening stock of 102,500.77 is completely inconsistent with a department generating weekly sales of 1,284.42; this is almost certainly a misfiled stock count or system posting error and requires immediate correction - do not allow this to roll into next week's opening stock.

Beef: Scan margin of 20.51% is the strongest in the meat aisle and waste of 84.07 is well controlled; ordering and rotation practices in this section should be noted as a benchmark for other meat sub-departments.

Lamb: Negative CoS of -30.33 and a margin of 101.91% are not credible; closing stock of 1,305.81 exceeding opening stock of 1,275.48 with no visible purchase receipts posted suggests either a purchase delivery was not booked in or the stock count is overstated.

Pork: Scan margin of 20.65% is healthy; waste of 81.87 is acceptable. The reported P&L margin of 87.84% remains overstated versus scan but the department is trending in the right direction relative to other meat categories.

Bacon & Loose S.R.P: Negative CoS of -201.47 and a margin of 117.90% are not credible. The scan margin of 11.62% is the lowest in the meat group and should be investigated for potential scan or pricing file issues in addition to the stock count anomaly.

Poultry: Highest sales volume department in the breakdown at 25,390.16; waste of 202.38 is proportionate to turnover. The scan margin of 16.41% is slightly below category target and should be monitored; check for any promotional mix impact on achieved margin.

Fish: Waste of 862.65 is disproportionate to sales of 6,083.82; the department manager must review end-of-day markdown procedures, production scheduling, and whether ordering is being adjusted in line with actual weekly demand patterns.

Produce: Negative CoS and a closing stock higher than opening without posted purchases requires reconciliation; the scan margin of 19.13% is a more reliable indicator of true department performance this week.

Deli: Missing closing stock is a fundamental reporting failure; combined with waste of 1,253.18 and a -149.61% margin this department needs both an immediate stock count and a production waste review before next week's trading commences.

Provisions: The waste figure of 1,136.11 on sales of 4,358.19 is the most urgent operational issue outside of data integrity problems; a line-by-line waste log review with the department manager should be scheduled for the coming week.
action_plan: Cigarettes - URGENT: Conduct a full physical stock count of the Cigarettes department before the next trading day and reconcile against the opening stock value of 102,500.77; identify whether this is a misfiled count, a system duplication, or an unresolved prior week carry-forward and correct the posting immediately.

Deli - URGENT: Complete and post a closing stock count for the Deli department for week ending 2026-05-16; without this the CoS is wholly inaccurate. Also review the waste log of 1,253.18 line by line with the Deli manager and identify the top five wasted lines by value.

Provisions: Schedule a meeting with the Provisions manager this week to review the waste figure of 1,136.11 in detail; identify whether this is production waste, date code clearance, or ordering over-supply and introduce a daily waste recording discipline with corrective ordering adjustments effective immediately.

Fish: Review the Fish department waste figure of 862.65 against the weekly order quantities and production schedule; implement end-of-day markdown triggers at a minimum of three hours before close and adjust the weekly order by a minimum of 10% downward pending further review.

Lamb and Bacon & Loose S.R.P: Investigate negative CoS figures in both departments (-30.33 and -201.47 respectively); confirm whether purchase deliveries were received but not booked in, or whether closing stock counts are overstated, and correct before these values roll into next week's opening figures.

Produce: Reconcile the Produce department stock position; confirm all purchase deliveries for the week have been posted against the department and recount closing stock if necessary to resolve the negative CoS of -793.52.

Scan Margins: Request a full scan margin vs P&L margin comparison report for all fresh departments from the category or IT support team; where the gap exceeds 20 percentage points a root cause analysis on stock counts, purchase postings, and wastage recording must be completed.

Waste overall: Total waste of 8,111.35 at 2.39% of sales is above the acceptable threshold; hold a cross-departmental waste review meeting with all fresh department managers this week, focusing on Deli, Provisions, Fish, and Produce which together account for 4,645.76 or 57.3% of total store waste.

Bacon & Loose S.R.P scan margin: At 11.62% this is significantly below the meat department average; check the pricing file for all Bacon and Loose S.R.P lines to confirm selling prices are correctly mapped and that no promotional pricing has been left active in error.

Poultry scan margin: Monitor the 16.41% scan margin in Poultry this coming week; review promotional mix and confirm that all poultry lines are scanning at the correct retail price, particularly any multi-buy or meal deal promotional SKUs.

Stock count integrity: Implement a manager sign-off protocol for all department stock counts before they are posted to the system; no count should be submitted without a second check by a duty manager where the resulting P&L margin falls outside a defined acceptable range, suggested at minus 5% to plus 40%.

Overall margin: The achieved store margin of 17.53% must be benchmarked against budget and prior year equivalent week; once the Cigarettes and Deli data errors are corrected, rerun the weekly P&L and present the restated margin to the store manager before the next management review meeting.
